description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a rugged, angular form and a predominantly pinkish-brown coloration. Its surface is marked by patches of pale yellow lichen or mineral deposits, giving it a mottled appearance. The rock features a combination of jagged edges, flat planes, and some smoother, weathered areas, suggesting exposure to natural elements over time. The texture appears coarse and gritty, with visible crevices and protrusions that would make it difficult to sit or stand on comfortably. Its mass and density are consistent with a solid stone, likely composed of granite or a similar hard rock. This type of rock could serve as a natural obstacle or decorative element in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: A rugged, pinkish-brown rock with angular edges and pale yellow lichen patches, featuring a coarse, weathered surface and irregular shape.
description_view_0: This view shows a broad, flat face with prominent yellowish spots and a jagged lower edge.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the rock's bulkier, more rounded top and a concentration of lichen patches.
description_view_2: Here, the rock appears flatter and more elongated, with visible layering and a rough, uneven surface.
description_view_3: This side reveals a steep, triangular profile with fewer lichen patches and a darker, more solid appearance.
